name: Golghar
description: A historic beehive-shaped granary built in 1786 with panoramic city views from its spiral staircase.

name: Patna Sahib Gurudwara
description: One of the five Takhts of Sikhism, marking the birthplace of Guru Gobind Singh Ji.

name: Mahavir Mandir
description: One of the most revered Hindu temples in Bihar dedicated to Lord Hanuman near Patna Junction.

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree.

name: Vishnupad Temple
description: Ancient temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu, built along the Falgu River with a sacred footprint on basalt.

name: Badi Dargah
description: A revered Sufi shrine known for its beautiful architecture and spiritual significance in North Bihar.

name: Ranchi Lake
description: Scenic artificial lake in the heart of Ranchi, perfect for boating surrounded by lush greenery.

name: Hundru Falls
description: A stunning 98-meter waterfall formed by the Subarnarekha River, one of the highest in Jharkhand.

name: Canary Hill
description: Hilltop viewpoint with stunning panoramic views of Hazaribagh town and surrounding forests.

name: Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A lush wildlife sanctuary home to tigers, leopards, and various deer species in dense sal forests.

name: Maithon Dam
description: Massive dam on the Barakar River surrounded by hills and dense forests with boating facilities.

name: Golden Temple
description: Sri Harmandir Sahib, the holiest Sikh shrine with its stunning golden dome and sacred pool.

name: Jallianwala Bagh
description: Historic memorial garden commemorating the 1919 massacre, with bullet marks still on the walls.

name: Rock Garden
description: Unique 40-acre sculpture garden created by Nek Chand using recycled industrial waste.

name: Sukhna Lake
description: Beautiful man-made reservoir at the Shivalik foothills, popular for boating and jogging.

name: Lodhi Fort
description: A Mughal-era fort built by Sikandar Lodhi offering historical significance and architectural charm.

name: India Gate
description: 42-meter tall war memorial arch, a tribute to 70,000 Indian soldiers who died in World War I.

name: Red Fort
description: UNESCO World Heritage Mughal fortress from 1638, symbol of India's sovereignty.

name: Qutub Minar
description: 73-meter UNESCO World Heritage minaret from 1193, the tallest brick minaret in the world.

name: Gateway of India
description: Iconic arch monument from 1924 overlooking the Arabian Sea, the most photographed Mumbai landmark.

name: Marine Drive
description: 3.6 km coastal promenade known as the 'Queen's Necklace' for its glittering nighttime arc.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus
description: UNESCO World Heritage Victorian Gothic railway station, an architectural masterpiece built in 1888.

name: Shaniwar Wada
description: 18th-century Peshwa fortification, the former seat of the Maratha Empire rulers.

name: Aga Khan Palace
description: Historic 1892 palace where Mahatma Gandhi was imprisoned, now a shrine to his legacy.

name: Deekshabhoomi
description: Sacred Buddhist stupa where Dr. Ambedkar embraced Buddhism, inspired by Sanchi architecture.

name: Lalbagh Botanical Garden
description: 240-acre garden with rare plants, a Crystal Palace-inspired glasshouse, and a 3-billion-year-old rock.

name: Bangalore Palace
description: Tudor-style 1878 palace inspired by Windsor Castle with Gothic fortified towers.

name: Mysore Palace
description: Stunning Indo-Saracenic palace of the Wadiyar dynasty, illuminated by 97,000 bulbs on Sundays.

name: Chamundi Hills
description: 1,065-meter hilltop with the Chamundeshwari Temple and massive Nandi Bull carved from a single rock.

name: Marina Beach
description: India's longest natural urban beach stretching 13 km with vibrant sunrise and sunset views.

name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple
description: Beautiful Dravidian temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, known for its stunning gopuram tower.

name: Marudhamalai Temple
description: Hilltop Lord Murugan temple perched on the Western Ghats with scenic views and spiritual serenity.

name: Victoria Memorial
description: Breathtaking white marble monument from 1921 blending British and Mughal architecture.

name: Howrah Bridge
description: Iconic cantilever bridge over the Hooghly River, one of the busiest bridges in the world.

name: Belur Math
description: Ramakrishna Mission HQ founded by Swami Vivekananda, its architecture symbolizes unity of religions.

name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple
description: Historic 19th-century Kali temple on the Hooghly River, associated with Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa.

name: Bara Imambara
description: 18th-century Mughal monument famous for its Bhool Bhulaiya labyrinth, one of the world's largest.

name: Rumi Darwaza
description: Imposing 60-foot gateway built in 1784 in Awadhi style, an enduring symbol of Lucknow.

name: Allen Forest Zoo
description: One of India's oldest zoos set in lush forest surroundings, home to diverse wildlife species.

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of 12 Jyotirlingas dedicated to Lord Shiva in India's spiritual capital with grand corridor.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat
description: The most famous Ganges ghat, known for its spectacular evening Ganga Aarti ceremony.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: Iconic 'Palace of Winds' with 953 small windows, a masterpiece of Rajput architecture.

name: Amber Fort
description: Majestic hill fort blending Rajput and Mughal styles with the stunning Sheesh Mahal mirror palace.

name: City Palace
description: Sprawling palace complex on Lake Pichola banks, blending Rajasthani and Mughal architecture.

name: Lake Pichola
description: Beautiful artificial freshwater lake surrounded by palaces and temples, home to the Lake Palace hotel.

name: Mehrangarh Fort
description: One of India's largest forts perched 400ft above the Blue City with excellent museum and views.

name: Sabarmati Ashram
description: Mahatma Gandhi's home from 1917-1930 and base for the Salt March, now a museum.

name: Adalaj Stepwell
description: Stunning five-story stepwell from 1498 with intricate carvings, an Indo-Islamic masterpiece.

name: Laxmi Vilas Palace
description: One of the grandest private residences in the world, four times the size of Buckingham Palace.

name: Dumas Beach
description: Serene Arabian Sea beach known for its unique black sand and stunning sunset views.

name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
description: UNESCO World Heritage baroque masterpiece from 1605 housing remains of St. Francis Xavier.

name: Fort Aguada
description: 17th-century Portuguese fort with a lighthouse offering spectacular coastal views.

name: Colva Beach
description: One of Goa's longest and most beautiful white-sand beaches stretching over 2.4 km.

name: Fort Kochi Beach
description: Charming beach with iconic Chinese fishing nets, colonial architecture, and vibrant art scene.

name: Mattancherry Palace
description: 16th-century Dutch Palace with stunning Kerala mural paintings depicting Hindu mythology.

name: Padmanabhaswamy Temple
description: Ancient Hindu temple known for Dravidian architecture and immense treasure vault.

name: Napier Museum
description: 19th-century museum with Indo-Saracenic architecture housing art, sculptures, and artifacts.

name: Tea Gardens
description: Endless rolling hills carpeted with emerald green tea plantations, Munnar's signature landscape.

name: Eravikulam National Park
description: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr with rolling grasslands and Western Ghats beauty.

name: The Ridge
description: Large open space with panoramic Himalayan views and the iconic Christ Church.

name: Jakhu Temple
description: Ancient temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman atop Jakhu Hill with a 108-foot tall Hanuman statue.

name: Solang Valley
description: Stunning valley for adventure sports — skiing, paragliding, zorbing against snow-capped peaks.

name: Hadimba Temple
description: Ancient cave temple surrounded by cedar forest, dedicated to Hidimbi Devi with unique pagoda architecture.

name: McLeod Ganj
description: Home of the Dalai Lama and Tibetan government-in-exile, blending Tibetan culture with Himalayan beauty.

name: Robber's Cave
description: Natural cave formation with a river flowing through narrow gorges — walk through cold water inside.

name: Sahastradhara
description: Thousand-fold spring with sulphur water springs cascading down limestone stalactites in a beautiful valley.

name: Har Ki Pauri
description: Most sacred Ganges ghat, famous for its spectacular evening Ganga Aarti ceremony.

name: Laxman Jhula
description: Famous suspension bridge over the Ganges, a spiritual and adventure hub of Uttarakhand.

name: Beatles Ashram
description: Former ashram of Maharishi Mahesh Yogi where The Beatles stayed in 1968, now an artistic landmark.

name: Sanchi Stupa
description: UNESCO World Heritage Buddhist monument built by Emperor Ashoka, one of India's oldest structures.

name: Upper Lake
description: One of India's oldest man-made lakes giving Bhopal its 'City of Lakes' nickname.

name: Rajwada Palace
description: 200-year-old Holkar palace blending Maratha, Mughal, and French architectural styles.

name: Charminar
description: Iconic 16th-century monument with four grand arches and minarets, heart of old city bazaar culture.

name: Golconda Fort
description: Medieval fortress famous for acoustic architecture, diamond mines, and sound-and-light show.

name: Warangal Fort
description: 13th-century Kakatiya dynasty fort with impressive stone gateways and the Thousand Pillar Temple.

name: Kailasagiri
description: Hilltop park with panoramic Bay of Bengal views, massive Shiva-Parvati statue, and ropeway ride.

name: Submarine Museum
description: INS Kurusura, India's first submarine museum housed in a real decommissioned submarine.

name: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
description: The richest and most-visited temple in the world, dedicated to Lord Venkateswara on Tirumala Hills.

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage 13th-century temple shaped as a Sun God chariot with 24 carved wheels.

name: Lingaraj Temple
description: 11th-century Odia-style architectural masterpiece, the largest and most important temple in Bhubaneswar.

name: Jagannath Temple
description: One of the four sacred Char Dhams, famous for its annual Rath Yatra with massive wooden chariots.

name: Puri Beach
description: Golden Bay of Bengal beach famous for annual sand art festival and vibrant seafood culture.

name: Chitrakoot Falls
description: 'Niagara of India' — horseshoe-shaped waterfall on Indravati River, the widest in India.

name: Kamakhya Temple
description: Ancient Shakti Peeth atop Nilachal Hill, one of India's most powerful tantric temples.

name: Umananda Island
description: World's smallest river island on the Brahmaputra with a Shiva temple and golden langur monkeys.

name: Kangla Fort
description: Ancient fortification and traditional seat of Manipur's rulers with temples and Kangla Sha dragons.

name: Loktak Lake
description: The largest freshwater lake in northeast India with unique floating phumdis (vegetation islands).

name: Living Root Bridges
description: Natural bridges grown from rubber fig tree roots by Khasi people over centuries, UNESCO nominee.

name: Elephant Falls
description: Beautiful three-tiered waterfall in East Khasi Hills named after an elephant-shaped rock.

name: Tsomgo Lake
description: Glacial lake at 12,310 ft surrounded by snow-capped mountains, color changes with the seasons.

name: Nathula Pass
description: Historic mountain pass at 14,140 ft on the Indo-China border with breathtaking views.

name: Ujjayanta Palace
description: Stunning Mughal-style 1901 palace, now Tripura State Museum surrounded by a Mughal Garden.

name: Ita Fort
description: Ancient 14th-century brick fort whose name gives Itanagar ('City of Bricks') its name.

name: Kohima War Cemetery
description: Beautifully maintained WWII memorial honoring soldiers of the pivotal Battle of Kohima.

name: Durtlang Hills
description: Stunning viewpoint on Aizawl's outskirts with panoramic views of the entire city and valleys.

name: Karna Lake
description: Historical lake named after the Mahabharata warrior Karna, a popular picnic and recreation spot.

name: Kingdom of Dreams
description: India's first live entertainment destination with Bollywood musicals and cultural shows.

name: Cyber Hub
description: Premium dining and entertainment district in DLF CyberCity with over 50 culinary experiences.
